description: In 2012, Kansas Governor Sam Brownback signed the Kansas Senate Bill 9, implementing massive tax cuts based on supply-side theory. By 2017, the resulting budget deficits and public service cuts led the Republican-led legislature to overturn the cuts, effectively declaring the experiment a policy failure.

key_events.description: Bill passes with strong initial support from conservative voters.
Standard & Poor's downgrades Kansas credit rating due to budget shortfalls.
Major school funding litigation highlights impacts of budget cuts.
State legislature overrides veto to repeal tax cuts.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring the erosion of support for economic theories when real-world fiscal data contradicts stated goals.
